_metadata.title: ComplianceLint Interim Standard for EU AI Act Article 27: Fundamental Rights Impact Assessment
_metadata.disclaimer: This is an interim standard developed by ComplianceLint. It will be superseded when CEN-CENELEC publishes the harmonized standard for fundamental rights impact assessment.
basis: EU AI Act Recitals 94-96 (legislative intent on fundamental rights impact assessment)
GDPR Article 35 -- Data protection impact assessment
_metadata.awaiting_standard: CEN-CENELEC harmonized standard for fundamental rights impact assessment (expected Q4 2026)

requirements.title: FRIA Documentation
requirements.requirement: The deployer MUST perform and document a fundamental rights impact assessment covering all six required elements: (a) description of processes, (b) period/frequency, (c) categories of persons affected, (d) specific risks, (e) human oversight measures, (f) risk materialisation measures.
requirements.rationale: Art. 27(1) mandates a comprehensive FRIA covering six specific elements for qualifying deployers.
required_content: Description of deployer processes using the AI system
Period of time and frequency of intended use
Categories of natural persons and groups likely affected
Specific risks of harm to identified categories
Description of human oversight measures implemented
Measures to be taken when identified risks materialise

requirements.title: FRIA Timing and Updates
requirements.requirement: The deployer MUST complete the FRIA before first use and MUST update it when relevant elements change.
requirements.rationale: Art. 27(2) requires the assessment before first use and ongoing updates.
acceptable_implementations: FRIA document with completion date prior to deployment date
Periodic review schedule with trigger conditions for updates
Change management process linked to FRIA review

requirements.title: Authority Notification
requirements.requirement: The deployer MUST notify the relevant market surveillance authority of the FRIA results.
requirements.rationale: Art. 27(3) requires notification of assessment results to the authority.
acceptable_implementations: Formal submission to national market surveillance authority
Registered communication channel with the authority

requirements.title: DPIA Complementarity
requirements.requirement: Where a DPIA has been conducted under GDPR, the FRIA MUST complement it rather than duplicate it.
requirements.rationale: Art. 27(4) requires the FRIA to build upon existing GDPR DPIA where applicable.
acceptable_implementations: FRIA document referencing existing DPIA and extending it with AI-specific elements
Integrated assessment document covering both GDPR and AI Act requirements
Cross-reference table mapping DPIA findings to FRIA elements
